properties.name: hard_core_min_distance
properties.statement: By construction of sequential hard-core acceptance, any two accepted points satisfy Euclidean distance >= epsilon (up to floating-point error).
properties.justification: Acceptance condition forbids adding a point within epsilon of an accepted one.

properties.name: diameter_bound_single_point
properties.statement: If epsilon > sqrt(d) (diameter of [0,1]^d), at most one point can be accepted.
properties.justification: Any two points in the unit cube are at distance <= sqrt(d).

properties.name: metric_not_ordinal
properties.statement: Selection depends only on Euclidean positions and epsilon, not on integer adjacency |i-j|; permuting labels without moving points leaves the geometry rule invariant.
properties.justification: Rule uses only X_i and ||·||_2; indices are tags.

properties.name: packing_cardinality_bound
properties.statement: Accepted set is an epsilon-packing of [0,1]^d; cardinality is O(epsilon^{-d}) (e.g. area argument with disjoint balls of radius epsilon/2 intersected with a slightly enlarged cube).
properties.justification: Standard packing bound for hard-core point sets in a bounded domain.

properties.name: density_decreases_with_epsilon
properties.statement: For 3/3 values of n, mean density strictly decreases as epsilon increases.
properties.justification: Wave-1 observation; consistent with packing but finite-n/seed.

properties.name: hard_core_numerical_check
properties.statement: Hard-core violations in raw min_distance < epsilon: 0 (expect 0).

properties.name: matérn_intensity_limit
properties.statement: Exact limiting intensity of this sequential thinning vs classical Matérn hard-core processes.
properties.justification: Not derived in wave-1; literature exists but not claimed here.

note: Intrinsic geometry only. Question: what MUST happen given this machine? Not: does it look like primes? No P* sealed.
